name: Cars & Automobiles
description: Classic and modern cars, brands, and motoring history

questions.q: Henry Ford's famous early 1900s automobile was known as the Model what?

source.notes: Produced 1908–1927; the first mass-produced car.

questions.q: Which German manufacturer built the iconic rounded 'Beetle' that sold over 21 million units?

source.notes: Original Beetle produced 1938–2003; the New Beetle ran until 2019.

questions.q: Porsche's iconic rear-engined sports car, in production since 1963, is named what?

source.notes: Nine-Eleven has been continuously produced for 60+ years.

questions.q: Ford's pony car, introduced in 1964 and named after a wild horse, is called the what?

source.notes: Debuted April 17, 1964 at the New York World's Fair.

questions.q: Toyota launched which pioneering mass-market hybrid car in 1997 in Japan (1999 in the U.S.)?

source.notes: 'Prius' is Latin for 'first' or 'prior'.

questions.q: Which Italian luxury sports car maker uses a prancing black horse on a yellow shield as its logo?

source.notes: Known as the 'Cavallino Rampante'. Ferrari is based in Maranello.

questions.q: Which German carmaker uses the slogan 'The Ultimate Driving Machine'?

source.notes: Used by BMW since 1975 in US advertising.

questions.q: Which 19th-century German engineer is credited with inventing the first practical automobile in 1885?

source.notes: Founded what became Mercedes-Benz after a 1926 merger.

questions.q: Henry Ford introduced the moving assembly line to automobile manufacturing in which year?

source.notes: At Ford's Highland Park plant in Michigan.

questions.q: Rolls-Royce's 1907 Silver Ghost is considered one of the greatest cars ever built. Which model famously succeeded it?

source.notes: Phantom I was introduced in 1925; multiple generations since.

questions.q: The Italian carmaker Maserati uses what symbol on its logo?

source.notes: Inspired by the trident in the statue of Neptune in Bologna.

questions.q: Which Japanese automaker's logo features three red diamonds?

source.notes: 'Mitsubishi' means 'three diamonds' in Japanese.

questions.q: Which model is often cited as the best-selling car nameplate of all time?

source.notes: Over 50 million produced since its 1966 debut.

questions.q: Tesla launched its first production car, the Roadster, in which year?

source.notes: Based on a modified Lotus Elise chassis.

questions.q: The famous Aston Martin DB5 is most associated with which film franchise?

source.notes: First appeared in 'Goldfinger' (1964).

questions.q: Since 1998, the luxury British carmaker Bentley has been owned by which group?

source.notes: Part of Volkswagen Group alongside Audi, Porsche, and Lamborghini.

questions.q: Ferrari is headquartered in which Italian town in the Emilia-Romagna region?

source.notes: Home of the Ferrari factory since 1943.

questions.q: Which muscle car plays Bumblebee in the live-action 'Transformers' films?

source.notes: In the original G1 cartoon, Bumblebee was a VW Beetle.

questions.q: General Motors was founded in 1908 in which Michigan city?

source.notes: Founded by William C. Durant in Flint, where Chevrolet was also founded.

questions.q: The 1954 Mercedes-Benz 300SL is famous for having what distinctive type of doors?

source.notes: Also called 'butterfly doors'; reused on the DeLorean DMC-12.

questions.q: ThrustSSC was the first automobile to break the sound barrier — in what year?

source.notes: Driven by RAF pilot Andy Green in Nevada's Black Rock Desert on October 15, 1997.

questions.q: Which Swedish car manufacturer was the first to introduce the modern three-point seat belt as standard in 1959?

source.notes: Designed by Volvo engineer Nils Bohlin; patent made freely available to save lives.
